free_text: Laboratori Nazionali di Legnaro.
Private communication of
U.Abbondanno with data and experimental details.
Preliminary report.
A study of the Co-59(n,n'gamma)Co-59 reaction
7 MV Van de Graaff, pulsed beam,
repetition rate 3 MHz.
Protons impinging on a titanium absorbed
tritium target. 0.84 Ci/cm**2.
99.9% pure natural metallic Co, 131 gram,
in a right cylinder of 2.9 diam, 6 cm height.
Time of flight with a pulsed beam.
Flight path 60 cm. Proton pulse width 3 ns.
This was used to discriminate between gammas and
scattered neutrons.
The charge collection was monitored by means of a
current integrator.
52 cm3 true coaxial Ge(Li) detector. Time
resolution 4 ns, energy resolution from 2.0 to
3.5 keV at 1170. keV.
NE213 scintillation counter for neutron
flux monitoring. At 4m from the neutron source
at 90 degrees with respect to the proton beam.
.An automatic spectrum analysis was used, see
Yield of the
846. keV and 1240. keV lines at 90. degrees, and
at 2.5 MeV incoming neutron energy. The values
are taken from INFN report BE-70/3.
.Systematic comparison of experimental excitation
function with statistical compound nucleus
calculations. See Phys.Rev. 87(1952)336 and
Rev.Mod.Phys. 38(1966)143.
.Multiple scattering following NIM,93(1971)103.
.neutron and gamma ray attenuation in the sample.
This error is the quadratical sum of
- the statistical error,
- the error in the determination of the gamma
ray detector efficiency,
- the error in the neutron monitoring and in the
cross section for the p-t reaction.
The error due to the absolute normalization
to the data from the Fe-56(n,inl) reaction.
The quadratic sum of DATA-ERR and ERR-1 is the total
error.
30 keV energy spread of the neutrons was
mainly due to the angular spread of the sample.
G.C.
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrected according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
.Data taken from private communication of
Abbondanno.
